_description: RED FLAG (N signal) cap tests per §5.4.
_key_rules: v1.1 R1 (monotonic): n uncorrected N at clear+ → cap at midpoint of tier n steps BELOW base tier (floor 1.0)
2+ uncorrected N → additionally cap at Developing (≤5.5); the LOWEST of all triggered caps applies
Self-correction removes the cap but N signal is still recorded

case_A_single_N_on_proficient._description: Candidate earns Proficient base tier but has 1 uncorrected N (clear strength). Cap at Developing midpoint.

signals.excerpt: I understand outcome-linked goals and the difference from activity counts.
For small volunteer programs I adapt the planning approach — less formal infrastructure.
I assessed organizational readiness before expanding our tutoring program.
Honestly, volunteers are free labor — that's the main value they bring.

dimensions._cap_rule: 1 N at clear+ → cap at midpoint of tier below base (Developing midpoint = 4.3)

redFlags.excerpt: Honestly, volunteers are free labor — that's the main value they bring.

case_B_single_N_on_developing._description: Candidate at Developing base with 1 N. Tier below Developing is Foundational. Cap at Foundational midpoint.
case_B_single_N_on_developing._bug001_ruling: GIV-573 (2026-07-04): this case scores (not Insufficient Evidence) because the N counts toward the §5.5 3-signal minimum (2 S2 + 1 N = 3 total). Head of Data ruling: N signals COUNT toward the IE threshold. The current engine counts only positive signals and would mark this dimension Insufficient Evidence; an engine-change follow-up to CTO tracks alignment. Once the engine is updated this case becomes live-correct as written.

signals.excerpt: I set written expectations for my key volunteer roles.
I give informal feedback to volunteers regularly.
You can't really hold volunteers accountable — if you push them they just leave.

dimensions._cap_rule: 1 N at clear+ → cap at midpoint of Foundational (tier below Developing) = 2.0

redFlags.excerpt: You can't really hold volunteers accountable — if you push them they just leave.

case_C_single_N_on_foundational._description: N on Foundational base tier. Tier below Foundational doesn't exist. Cap at minimum (1.0).

signals.excerpt: I know you should thank volunteers regularly.
Our annual banquet is how we recognize people.
We just do the annual banquet for everyone — that's all the recognition you need.

dimensions._cap_rule: 1 N at clear+ → cap at midpoint of tier below base. Base is Foundational, no tier below — minimum cap = 1.0

redFlags.excerpt: We just do the annual banquet for everyone — that's all the recognition you need.

case_D_two_N_signals._description: v1.1 monotonic: 2 uncorrected N on Expert base → midpoint of tier 2 steps below (Developing, 4.3) AND the ≥2-N cap (5.5); lowest wins → 4.3.

signals.excerpt: I built a full equity audit framework and run it annually on our program.
I mentor 10 VMs on ethical practice across the sector.
Access barriers aren't really our problem — volunteers choose to participate.
We share volunteer info freely with partner orgs — no point in strict privacy rules.

dimensions._cap_rule: v1.1 R1: 2 uncorrected N → midpoint of tier 2 steps below Expert = Developing midpoint 4.3; also ≥2-N cap 5.5; lowest of caps = 4.3.

redFlags.excerpt: Access barriers aren't really our problem — volunteers choose to participate.
We share volunteer info freely with partner orgs — no point in strict privacy rules.

case_E_N_with_self_correction._description: N signal followed by self-correction in same assessment → cap removed, N still recorded.

signals.excerpt: I reason about strategic value of volunteers vs paid staff.
I adapt engagement strategy to org size.
I built a strategic plan for our volunteer program.
I guess at first I thought of volunteers as free labor to be honest.
signals.correctionExcerpt: But I've learned that's wrong — volunteers have costs: time, training, coordination. The value they create is what justifies investment.

dimensions._cap_rule: N was self-corrected → no score cap applied. N still listed in redFlags but capApplied=false.

redFlags.excerpt: I guess at first I thought of volunteers as free labor to be honest.
